title: nohawilliamsia pirarensis
side_effects: Nohawilliamsia pirarensis can have side effects if used improperly, such as causing digestive issues, including diarrhea and stomach cramps, due to its high concentration of toxic alkaloids. Ingesting large quantities of the plant can also lead to nausea, vomiting, and dizziness, as well as exacerbate existing kidney problems. Furthermore, improper use of nohawkwilliamsia pirarensis may trigger allergic reactions, such as skin irritation, itching, and hives, in some individuals. In severe cases, overexposure to the plant's toxic compounds can cause more serious health issues, including organ failure and even death.
